whyFit: Best match for remote customer service + A+. Phone/chat/email support, remote troubleshooting, documentation, escalation.
typicalWork: Answer phone/chat/email requests; troubleshoot login, device, software, browser, printer, VPN, or basic network issues; use remote desktop tools; write clear ticket notes; escalate when needed.

role: Help Desk Analyst I / Service Desk Analyst
whyFit: Most direct A+ entry point. Password resets, hardware/software issues, ticketing, Windows, M365, basic network troubleshooting.
typicalWork: Password resets and account unlocks; Windows support; M365/Outlook/Teams support; hardware/peripheral troubleshooting; ticket triage; escalation to Tier 2.
searchTerms: entry level help desk CompTIA A+

whyFit: Great bridge from customer service into tech. Supports users of a software product without requiring deep infrastructure experience.
typicalWork: Help customers use a software product; answer workflow/setup questions; reproduce issues; escalate bugs; write help articles or internal notes.

whyFit: Higher-paying cousin of help desk. Recreates issues, writes tickets, tests fixes, supports business apps, talks to users.
typicalWork: Troubleshoot application issues; recreate errors; document user problems; test fixes; work with developers/administrators; help users understand workflows.

role: IT Asset Coordinator / IT Asset Analyst
whyFit: Good hidden entry point. Tracks laptops, software, inventory, imaging, shipping, repairs, lifecycle records.
typicalWork: Track laptops/monitors/phones/peripherals; update inventory records; coordinate shipping and returns; support device refresh projects; track software licenses; work with procurement and IT support.
